steps.title: Calculate the y-intercept
steps.content: The y-intercept occurs at $x=0$. A fast Desmos check is to make a one-row table with $x_1=0$ and evaluate the original function: $4^{-5(x_1+1)}$. Desmos returns $0.0009765625$, which is $\frac{1}{1024}$. So the y-coordinate of the y-intercept is $\frac{1}{1024}$.

steps.title: Rewrite the function using exponent rules
steps.content:
Expand the exponent in the original function:
$$4^{-5(x+1)} = 4^{-5x-5}$$
Use the rule $a^{m+n} = a^m \cdot a^n$ to separate the constant from the variable part:
$$4^{-5x-5} = 4^{-5} \cdot 4^{-5x}$$
Substitute $4^{-5} = \frac{1}{1024}$ and rewrite $4^{-5x}$ as a power of $\frac{1}{4}$:
$$4^{-5x} = (4^{-1})^{5x} = \left(\frac{1}{4}\right)^{5x}$$
Therefore:
$$f(x) = \frac{1}{1024} \cdot \left(\frac{1}{4}\right)^{5x}$$

steps.title: Identify the choice
steps.content: The rewritten form $\frac{1}{1024} \cdot \left(\frac{1}{4}\right)^{5x}$ displays $\frac{1}{1024}$ explicitly as a coefficient. If you want to use Desmos as a choice check, evaluate each form at $x=0$; they all give the same y-intercept, but only choice A visibly writes that y-intercept as a coefficient. Choice B and choice C hide it inside the exponent, and choice D uses $1,024$ instead of $\frac{1}{1024}$. That matches choice A, so A is correct.
